data.revised_prompt: Design specimen, single frame of an alternative website hero animation called Compilation Graph, for a programming language brand CHIBA. Aesthetic: academic compiler lab notebook, precise and quiet. Background: warm paper white (#FAFAF7). Composition: a directed acyclic graph diagram, like a compiler intermediate representation graph being drawn by hand. Nodes are small rectangles with 1px deep forest green (#1F6B3E) outline, each containing a short label in IBM Plex Mono: parse, typecheck, monomorphize, borrow check, codegen, asm. Edges are hairline 1px black bezier curves with small arrowheads. Several edges shown mid-draw, with a tiny green dot at the leading tip suggesting an animated stroke in progress. One node is highlighted with a subtle deep forest green fill at very low opacity (10%) to indicate the currently active compilation stage. Top label: HERO ANIMATION / COMPILATION GRAPH / 2 of 2. Bottom corner: small metric strip showing PASS 4/6 - 0.42s in mono. NO glow, NO gradients, NO shadows, NO 3D effects, NO matrix rain, NO cyberpunk. The diagram looks like a clean technical illustration from a Bell Labs paper.
